reason: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) genuinely ran with --write and applied edits, and its math/source verification was sound (it cloned the original cuda-convnet AlexNet source and grounded LRN/PCA/SGD constants against layers-145.cfg). But the rewrite FAILED re-verification: it deleted every runnable python code block from answer.md, reasoning.md, and the context.md scaffold (replacing them with prose + plain-text pseudocode), so the answer no longer contains a python block to ast-parse and no longer ends in working code like every peer method. It also rewrote the first-person in-frame reasoning into an out-of-frame 'strict artifact' that introduces exactly the posterior/source-reference leaks the audit is meant to remove (references to 'the released cuda-convnet parser', 'conv1a/conv1b', 'archived layers-145.cfg', 'modern single-stream PyTorch model', 'torchvision'), and added section headers when restructuring context.md. Because the transformation regressed the deliverables rather than fixing them in place, the three result files plus notes/synthesis.md were reverted to their exact pre-review state (sha256 verified identical to originals). No faithful in-place fix was produced.
